title: Request: Tab Titles to Follow Current SSH Session
author: ejtbrown
body_snippet:
|||
|--:|---|
|Operating system | Windows 10 x64 |
|mRemoteNG version| 1.75.7008.17907  |


<!-- Feature Request -->
I've previously used MTPuTTY, but have switched to mRemoteNG. I find mRemoteNG to be better in every way... except one. The titles of tabs in MTPuTTY follow SSH connections.

Example:
1) Open an SSH connection tab to hostA.example.com; the title of the tab is 'hostA.example.com'
2) On the command line of hostA, ssh to hostB.example.com

In mRemoteNG, the title of the ...

iterations.description: Feature request to dynamically update tab titles based on the active SSH session hostname/directory, improving UX for users with multiple SSH connections
Bulk verified: commit e7a3ac61 exists (MEDIUM confidence)

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

notes:
AI triage: Feature request to dynamically update tab titles based on the active SSH session hostname/directory, improving UX for users with multiple SSH connections
Approach: Monitor the SSH terminal emulator for shell prompt changes (hostname, pwd) and update the corresponding tab title in real-time, with an option to toggle between static connection name and dynamic session title
